async_execution.mechanism: Hooks are synchronous; each fires before the tool executes and must complete within the configured timeout.

context_injection.mechanism: The hook JSON response may include an optional context field; this content is injected into the agent context for the current turn.

decision_control.mechanism: Hook subprocess exit code controls tool execution: exit code 2 blocks the tool call (deny). Other exit codes allow it. The hook may also return a JSON decision field with values allow, deny, or none.

handler_types.mechanism: Hooks are shell commands (command field in HookConfig). Each hook fires a subprocess with the tool context passed on stdin.

hook_scopes.mechanism: Hooks are configured in crush.json under the hooks key, which applies at the project or user-global config level (same config file scope as MCP and other settings).

input_modification.mechanism: Hooks receive tool context on stdin and can return context to the agent, but modifying tool input parameters is not documented.

json_io_protocol.mechanism: Hooks receive a JSON object on stdin containing tool name and parameters. The hook subprocess may return a JSON object with decision (allow/deny/none) and optional context fields.

matcher_patterns.mechanism: HookConfig has a matcher field: a regex pattern tested against the tool name. An empty matcher matches all tools.

permission_control.mechanism: Hooks run before permission checks. Exit code 2 blocks the tool call entirely, taking effect before the built-in permission gate.

providerExtensions.name: Event-keyed hook map
providerExtensions.summary: The hooks config key is an object keyed by event name (e.g. PreToolUse), with each value being an array of HookConfig objects. This allows multiple hooks per event and per-tool matcher filtering.

providerExtensions.name: Per-hook timeout
providerExtensions.summary: Each HookConfig entry has a timeout field (integer, seconds, default 30) that limits how long the hook subprocess may run before being killed.

providerExtensions.name: Hooks fire before permission checks
providerExtensions.summary: Crush's hookedTool decorator runs hook commands before the built-in permission gate, giving hooks full veto power over tool calls independent of configured permissions.
